catalog_metadata.title: Bloomberg Terminal dashboard/tool/widget/function catalog — public-source structured taxonomy
catalog_metadata.scope_note: This is a structured best-effort catalog of dashboard-like Bloomberg Terminal functions. It includes every function explicitly named in the uploaded Quick Start PDF, adds the user-named rates/auction tools (GRM, CRVF, GAM, ECO GBA, DDIS), and extends the set with publicly documented fixed-income, macro, Launchpad, portfolio, derivatives and Excel/API workflows. Bloomberg has tens of thousands of commands and many entitlement-specific screens; this file should be treated as a public-source map rather than a complete proprietary function inventory.

primary_sources.label: uploaded_pdf:Bloomberg Terminal Quick Start (Top Newsroom Functions for the Terminal.pdf)
primary_sources.coverage: Quick Start pages, cheat-sheet functions, screenshots and descriptions

primary_sources.label: online:Bloomberg Getting Started Guide for Students
primary_sources.coverage: student function list, help/resources, all-asset examples

primary_sources.label: online:Bloomberg Econ and Global Macro Key Functionality
primary_sources.kind: public Bloomberg-style economics macro cheat sheet
primary_sources.coverage: macro, central-bank, FX, capital-flow and visualization functions

primary_sources.label: online:HBS Baker Library Bloomberg yield curves help
online:Bloomberg DDIS/CAST webinar page
primary_sources.coverage: DDIS/CAST corporate debt structure use case

primary_sources.label: user_provided_example
primary_sources.coverage: GRM, GAM and ECO GBA examples and desired scope

schema.code: Bloomberg mnemonic or synthetic catalog code for path-specific views/components.
schema.name: Human-readable widget/function name.
schema.aliases_or_paths: Alternate commands, related paths or common variants.
schema.asset_class: One or more broad asset-class/workflow groups.
schema.widget_type: Enumerated UI/function type, e.g. market_monitor, calendar, screener, curve_tool, pricer, map.
schema.input_requirement: Whether the function is standalone, needs a loaded security, or is a path/subview.
schema.primary_views: Dominant UI outputs: table, chart, map, calendar, matrix/heatmap, portal, workspace.
schema.data_domains: Data families included in the screen.
schema.analytics_modes: Main analytic jobs the screen supports.
schema.display_columns_or_measures: Canonical columns/measures visible or likely available.
schema.user_actions: What users commonly do in the screen.
schema.source_notes: Public/source breadcrumbs used to create the record.

generic_component_templates.description: Track security lists with custom columns, color coding, alerts and Excel/list imports.
Headlines/research component powered by all-news, custom filters, news subjects, subject pairs or linked monitors.
Single- or multi-security charts, two-security spreads, market-picture histogram and technical studies.
Many traditional Terminal functions can be converted into Launchpad components using LLP <GO>.

functions.name: Bloomberg Launchpad
aliases_or_paths: LLP <GO> can convert many Terminal functions into Launchpad components
functions.freeform_description: Workspace layer for assembling Bloomberg functions and smaller components into user-defined views/pages. Supports monitors, news panels, charts, grouped components, sample views and workflow-specific layouts.
source_notes: online:Bloomberg Launchpad getting-started guides

functions.name: Launchpad Chart Component
aliases_or_paths: G <GO> charts can be stored/used by Chart Manager
functions.freeform_description: Launchpad chart component for single-security, multiple-security, two-security spread and histogram charting. Can incorporate technical studies and is related to G <GO> charting.
source_notes: online:Bloomberg Launchpad getting-started guides

functions.name: Launchpad Monitor Component
